description: §51.0.H (S182) — `effect=` is a §7 logic-context BLOCK; the `${...}` delimiter is MANDATORY. A bare `effect=log("go")` (the plain event-handler sugar `onclick=load()` of §5.2.3) does NOT extend to `effect=` and is NOT a silent no-op → E-ENGINE-EFFECT-NOT-INTERPOLATED. The fix is to wrap the body: `effect=${ log("go") }` (the form every existing lifecycle runtime case uses). Complements `effect-ambiguous-multi-target`: this one is a well-placed single-target effect that is only mis-DELIMITED.
rationale: §51.0.H: `effect=` is a §7 logic-context block (a full, possibly multi-statement body — codegen re-parses it as `${...}`), so the delimiter is mandatory. A bare `effect=playSound(...)` or unbalanced/empty braces is E-ENGINE-EFFECT-NOT-INTERPOLATED, NOT a silent no-op. The bare single-expression sugar that a plain event handler permits does NOT extend to `effect=`.
